desc: Seville is Andalusia's flagship city break for palace architecture, cathedral landmarks, flamenco heritage, and long evening street life. It is busiest in spring and autumn when weather is comfortable and festival demand is strongest; midsummer remains lively but shifts toward late-night schedules due to extreme daytime heat.
hls: Real Alcázar, Cathedral, and Giralda landmarks
Tapas culture across Santa Cruz, Arenal, and Alameda
Guadalquivir waterfront walks and river sunsets
Excellent rail links to Córdoba, Madrid, and Cádiz

todo.name: Real Alcázar timed visit
todo.desc: Tour intricate Mudéjar courtyards and lush palace gardens; prebook to avoid long queues.

todo.name: Cathedral and Giralda climb
todo.desc: See Columbus-related exhibits and climb the former minaret ramp for city panoramas.

todo.name: Barrio Santa Cruz wandering
todo.desc: Explore shaded lanes, hidden patios, and historic plazas in the old Jewish quarter.

todo.name: Triana market and flamenco night
todo.desc: Combine local food stalls with an evening performance in the neighborhood tied to flamenco traditions.

todo.name: Plaza de España and María Luisa Park
todo.desc: Visit Seville’s grand semicircular landmark and relax in adjacent gardens.

todo.name: Tapas crawl around Alameda or Arenal
todo.desc: Sample salmorejo, montaditos, and seasonal Andalusian plates across multiple bars.

wifi.note: Strong in central hotels, serviced apartments, and coworking cafés; daytime reliability is good, though outdoor terrace usage can be slower in peak evening hours.

prac.fltNote: Gatwick–Seville typically has regular nonstop capacity, especially in spring/autumn demand periods. Return fares are often ~£40–£100 off-peak, ~£100–£170 in shoulder demand, and ~£170+ around Easter/Feria weeks, summer holidays, and Christmas peaks.
prac.visa: Spain is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally visit visa-free up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: July–August daytime heat can exceed safe comfort for long outdoor visits; use early starts and siesta timing.
Major spring event periods (Semana Santa/Feria) can sharply increase hotel and flight prices.
Real Alcázar timed entries can sell out early during peak months; prebook essential sites.

sweet: March to May and October to early November offer the best balance of pleasant temperatures, event atmosphere, and manageable logistics.
